name: Keep the export-path gain and narrow the active PTX consumer-side B reuse

summary.name: Keep the export-path gain and narrow the active PTX consumer-side B reuse
summary.hypothesis: Primary family for round 39/100: Bank Conflict plus Register Reuse plus Ps2r on the active 128x128 K16 PTX hot-band branch. Round 38 proved the padded PTX export scratch was real upside because runtime improved to 25.974272 ms, a new best custom result only 0.056383 ms behind the 25.917889 ms CUTLASS baseline. But the run still sat on top of the earlier consumer-side B-reuse changes, and the stall mix stayed awkward instead of cleanly better: tensor-active was 47.87%, barrier stayed high at 11.13%, short-scoreboard stayed high at 5.87%, and mio_throttle remained very low at 0.55%. That combination says the export-path win should be kept, but the current two-row-pair B-fragment fusion is still probably too wide for the active PTX branch. The next best move is therefore a narrower warp-local consumer fallback rather than another broad rewrite: keep the mirrored consume order and the export scratch improvement, but shorten B-fragment lifetime or reuse scope so the branch gives back some short-scoreboard and barrier cost without surrendering the mio-throttle win.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Warp-local dependency chains and synchronization exposure in the active PTX consume path, where the current B-fragment reuse shape keeps feed pressure low but still leaves short-scoreboard and barrier cost too high.
summary.risk: Medium. Narrowing the current reuse pattern is bounded and stays on the active PTX branch, but it can easily give back the mio-throttle win or reduce tensor issue if the fallback becomes too timid.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the narrower reuse fallback gives back most of the 0.55% mio-throttle result, or if runtime does not improve while short-scoreboard and barrier remain near the current 5.87% and 11.13% levels.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
